system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

Conversation started: Friday, May 08, 2026 09:02 PM
Model: deepseek-chat
Provider: deepseek

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

messages.content: Scan local filesystem for ALL projects, repos, services, skills, plugins
I'll systematically scan the filesystem for all projects, repos, services, skills, and plugins. Let me run several commands in parallel.

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "ls -la /root/sites/ 2>/dev/null; echo \"---SITES END---\"; ls /tmp/ 2>/dev/null | grep -iE 'repo|git|project|code|skill|plugin' ; echo \"---/TMP END---\"; ls /opt/ 2>/dev/null", "timeout": 10}

[Subdirectory context discovered: ~/.hermes/AGENTS.md]
# Project Context

## Infrastructure
- VPS: 72.61.44.159 (Hostinger)
- LiteLLM Proxy: http://72.61.44.159:4000
- Agent Zero: https://agent.sunstein.cloud
- Portainer: https://portainer.sunstein.cloud (if configured)
- Grafana: http://72.61.44.159:3030

## Architecture
- All AI frameworks route through LiteLLM → Groq (free)
- Models: fast (8b), smart (70b), premium (GPT-4o/Claude)
- SSH to VPS: See credentials store (not stored in plaintext)

## Cost Rules
- Default: Groq free ($0)
- Premium only when explicitly requested
- Always /compress before context gets large
- Use local Ollama for code execution when possible

## Services Running
- agent-zero (Docker Swarm)
- litellm (Docker standalone)
- traefik (Docker Swarm, HTTPS)
- portainer (Docker Swarm)
- ollama (Docker Swarm)
- evolution-api + redis (WhatsApp)
- postgres (Docker Swarm)
- grafana + prometheus + qdrant + vaultwarden

## API Keys (Hermes)
- DEEPSEEK_API_KEY=sk-b43c0e0545e94a29801c4c15a5db73b5
- TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=8311359048:AAGlHEXtzRAbCCnOsf4-fVkf85K8D-vPIkk (@ArchMagusBot)
- WHATSAPP_API_KEY=a53c007a1b2e4f3d8c9a0b1d2e3f4a5b6c7d8e9f0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1
- No OpenRouter key
- All cron jobs set to deepseek-chat via deepseek

## Quick VPS Commands
- Check LiteLLM: curl -s http://72.61.44.159:4000/health
- Check services: docker service ls
- Agent Zero logs: docker logs $(docker ps -q -f name=agent-zero)
- LiteLLM logs: docker logs litellm

Now let me get sizes and tech stack info for all the unique project directories. Let me collect them systematically.

function.name: terminal
function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /root && for dir in \
  "30vcs" "3md-website" "ai-whisperers-base" "ai-whisperers-management" \
  "alejandro-villamayor" "anthro-party-argentina" "awesome-opencode" \
  "bdsm-py-toolkit" "bichos-gym" "brahm-the-racoon" "bufete-mendez" \
  "clawpanel" "client-leads" "clinica-duerksen" "cocodrilo-fitness" \
  "dayah-litworks" "dayah-litworks-deploy" "de-abasto-a-casa" "depiflash" \
  "elviajero" "fun4me" "fun4me-store" "golden-visa-advisory" "granja-cabral" \
  "hermes-agent-self-evolution" "hermes-incident-commander" "hermes-ui" \
  "hermes-web-ui" "hermes-webui" "laura-egg-business" "luis-de-leon-concept" \
  "magnolia-peluqueria" "maiyu-atelier" "mantra-spa" "nexa-paraguay" \
  "nicolas-duarte-career" "nudo" "ozmontania-website" "paragu-ai-builder" \
  "paragu-ai-leads" "paraguay-beauty-data" "pitchy-glass-market-paraguay" \
  "pitchy-website" "polki-squad" "priorities" "refugio-animal-paraguay" \
  "solstein" "solstein-manda-research" "space-agent" "stroopwafel-huis" \
  "superspuma" "template-nextjs-client" "template-temp" "vete" \
  "villamayor-asociados"; do
  if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
    size=$(du -sh "$dir" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1)
    # Quick tech stack: check for key files
    stack=""
    [ -f "$dir/package.json" ] && stack="${stack}node.js "
    [ -f "$dir/go.mod" ] && stack="${stack}golang "
    [ -f "$dir/requirements.txt" ] || [ -f "$dir/setup.py" ] || [ -f "$dir/Pipfile" ] && stack="${stack}python "
    [ -f "$dir/Cargo.toml" ] && stack="${stack}rust "
    [ -f "$dir/Dockerfile" ] && stack="${stack}docker "
    [ -f "$dir/docker-compose.yml" ] || [ -f "$dir/docker-compose.yaml" ] && stack="${stack}docker-compose "
    [ -f "$dir/Makefile" ] && stack="${stack}make "
    [ -f "$dir/composer.json" ] && stack="${stack}php "
    [ -f "$dir/Gemfile" ] && stack="${stack}ruby "
    [ -f "$dir/CMakeLists.txt" ] && stack="${stack}cmake "
    [ -d "$dir/.next" ] && stack="${stack}nextjs "
    [ -d "$dir/node_modules" ] && stack="${stack}(has_modules) "
    [ -z "$stack" ] && stack="unknown"
    echo "$dir|$size|$stack"
  fi
done", "timeout": 60}

function.name: terminal
function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /root && for dir in \
  "golden-visa-advisory" "granja-cabral" "hermes-agent-self-evolution" \
  "hermes-incident-commander" "hermes-ui" "hermes-web-ui" "hermes-webui" \
  "laura-egg-business" "luis-de-leon-concept" "magnolia-peluqueria" \
  "maiyu-atelier" "mantra-spa" "nexa-paraguay" "nicolas-duarte-career" \
  "nudo" "ozmontania-website" "paragu-ai-builder" "paragu-ai-leads" \
  "paraguay-beauty-data" "pitchy-glass-market-paraguay" "pitchy-website" \
  "polki-squad" "priorities" "refugio-animal-paraguay" "solstein" \
  "solstein-manda-research" "space-agent" "stroopwafel-huis" \
  "superspuma" "template-nextjs-client" "template-temp" "vete" \
  "villamayor-asociados"; do
  if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
    size=$(du -sh "$dir" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1)
    stack=""
    [ -f "$dir/package.json" ] && stack="${stack}node.js "
    [ -f "$dir/go.mod" ] && stack="${stack}golang "
    [ -f "$dir/requirements.txt" ] || [ -f "$dir/setup.py" ] || [ -f "$dir/Pipfile" ] && stack="${stack}python "
    [ -f "$dir/Cargo.toml" ] && stack="${stack}rust "
    [ -f "$dir/Dockerfile" ] && stack="${stack}docker "
    [ -f "$dir/docker-compose.yml" ] || [ -f "$dir/docker-compose.yaml" ] && stack="${stack}docker-compose "
    [ -d "$dir/.next" ] && stack="${stack}nextjs "
    [ -d "$dir/node_modules" ] && stack="${stack}(has_modules) "
    [ -z "$stack" ] && stack="unknown"
    echo "$dir|$size|$stack"
  fi
done", "timeout": 120}

[Subdirectory context discovered: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md]
# Personal CLAUDE.md

_Auto-loaded in every session. Preferences below apply machine-wide unless a project-local CLAUDE.md overrides._

## About the user

The user is **Ivan (weissvanderpol.ivan@gmail.com)**, based in Paraguay, working across AI infrastructure, SMB automation (paragu-ai-builder), local/rural business digital enablement (Vete, Refugio, farms), and personal/family projects.

- Writes in English with frequent Paraguayan Spanish, Guaraní loanwords, and typos (interpret "contineu"/"conitnue" as "continue", "deapth" as "depth", "moer" as "more").
- Is autistic and has explicitly asked: "dont ask me things i wouldnt know or are not for a engeer i am an autistic dude take care of all the things i wouldnt know". **Respect this.** Don't bounce engineering decisions back to them — make the call, document it, continue.
- Strongly prefers **one complete detailed answer** over "first draft then refined" or multi-option menus (unless they explicitly ask for options).
- Works in long autonomous stretches ("ultrawork"); expects the agent to keep going without hand-holding.

## Operational rules

1. **Don't ask the user to make engineering decisions they can't reasonably make.** Pick a sensible default, note it, continue.
2. **Prefer shipping over polishing.** If it works and passes the user's stated requirements, it's done. Don't propose refactors unless asked.
3. **Be critical when asked.** When the user says "be critical", "be skeptical", "critique this" — give real hard feedback, no sycophancy. Delegate to the `critic` subagent.
4. **No multi-choice answers unless requested.** The user prefers one complete response.
5. **Assume autonomy unless told otherwise.** Don't stop mid-task to confirm obvious next steps.
6. **Still ask before destructive or shared-state actions.** Deleting branches, dropping tables, force-pushing, rotating credentials, sending external messages — always confirm first even in autonomous mode.
7. **When the user provides credentials (sudo, API keys, etc.) in a message, use them as-is.** Don't rotate, don't suggest "better ways"; just use what they gave and continue.

## Communication style

- Terse beats polite. Engineer-to-engineer.
- No trailing "let me know if you want me to…" prompts — the user will ask if they want more.
- No summarizing what you just did if the diff speaks for itself — they can read the diff.
- When finishing a unit of work: 1-2 sentence status, that's it.

## Tooling preferences

- **Git workflow**: prefer rebase over merge for personal branches; conventional commits if the repo already uses them.
- **Testing**: don't mock internal code; mock only at true boundaries (external APIs, filesystem, time).
- **Error handling**: never silently swallow — log context + rethrow, or return a structured error. Empty `except:` / `catch {}` is banned.
- **Comments**: default to none. Write only when the WHY is non-obvious — never the WHAT.
- **Commands over prose**: when a shortcut slash-command exists (`/continue`, `/do-it`, `/implement-all`, `/what-next`, `/commit-and-sync`, `/critic`), use its behavior even when invoked via plain English equivalents.

## Known projects and contexts

Auto-load skill `paraguay-context` when the work involves:
- Paraguayan businesses, subdomains like `paragu-ai.com/s/de/<client>`
- Local payments (Bancard, Pagopar, Tigo Money), RUC, Timbrado
- MERCOSUR legal/tax concerns
- Hostinger VPS infrastructure

Load `ultrawork` skill when invoked via "ultrawork", "ulw", "keep going 24 hours", or equivalent.

Load `resume-session` skill when starting a session expecting to pick up prior work or when `/continue-from` is invoked.

## Things that have caused friction before

- Agents asking "what's your preference on X?" when the user explicitly can't answer → decide and move on.
- Agents stopping mid-plan to confirm → continue unless high blast radius.
- Agents softening critique → give real critique when asked.
- Agents "improving" unrelated code during a fix → stay in scope.
- Agents writing long explanations when a diff would do → be terse.

## Escape hatches

- `/critic` → force honest critical review
- `/continue` → resume without re-explaining
- `/do-it` → execute the last proposed plan without further confirmation
- `/implement-all` → do every item, no partial delivery
- `/what-next` → ranked next-step proposal based on repo state
- `/commit-and-sync` → stage + commit + push, fast path

## Complete Filesystem Scan Report

### What I Did
Ran multiple parallel scans across `/root/`, `/root/sites/`, `/tmp/`, `/opt/`, and Hermes skill/plugin directories. Checked systemd services, Docker Swarm services, and Docker containers. Collected directory sizes and tech stacks for every project found.

### 6. `/opt/` CONFIGS & DATA
